item.title: France evacuates Cap Ferret peninsula and Spain’s Sanchez warns of ‘dramatic situation’ as wildfires spread – Europe live
item.author: Jakub Krupa
item.content:encoded: Evacuees have been told to leave using hundreds of mobilised boats or the only road linking the area with the mainlandOver in France, it’s not just the south-west that gets affected, but the country’s south-east regions are also being hard hit, AFP noted.Dozens of fires flared up and are being fanned by the mistral wind around the historic village of – officials saying around 25 houses had been destroyed since Tuesday.Continue reading...

item.title: Andy Burnham to spend first working day at new No 10 North in Manchester – UK politics live
item.author: Taz Ali
item.content:encoded: PM will chair the inaugural meeting of the national economic council that will aim to shift economic decision-making away ‌from LondonHeavy police presence this morning outside Heron House in central Manchester, where the prime minister will hold his first meeting at No 10 North:Consumer confidence in the UK jumped this month by its biggest margin for nearly three years as the prospect of Andy Burnham becoming prime minister became almost certain.Continue reading...

item.title: France orders total evacuation of Cap Ferret peninsula as wildfire spreads
item.author: Jon Henley Europe correspondent
item.content:encoded: ‘Intense and highly unpredictable’ blaze forces more than 40,000 people to flee popular holiday spotAuthorities in south-western France have ordered the total evacuation of the Cap Ferret peninsula on the Atlantic coast by land and sea as an “intense and highly unpredictable” wildfire advances on one of the country’s best-known holiday spots.The fire had burned more than 10,000 hectares (24,700 acres) by Friday morning, the French interior minister, Laurent Nuñez, said, with 40,000 people already or about to be evacuated, including thousands of tourists from campsites and rental homes.Continue reading...

item.title: University courses covering Israel-Palestine should be audited, Julian Leeser tells royal commission
item.author: Caitlin Cassidy
item.content:encoded: Inquiry’s focus this week is on security for the Jewish community, with the MP telling the commissioner his son is afraid to attend synagogues and eventsThe shadow education minister, Julian Leeser, has called for universities to undertake an audit of any classes that teach about the Jewish community, Israel or Palestine.Appearing before the royal commission into antisemitism and social cohesion on Friday, Leeser said he had been “very concerned” about antisemitism on university campuses and was not satisfied with how vice-chancellors had addressed the issue after the 7 October 2023 Hamas terrorist attack.Continue reading...

item.title: Julian Leeser says his son no longer feels safe at Jewish events – as it happened
item.author: Achol Arok (now) and Catie McLeod and Kat Wong (earlier)
item.content:encoded: Australian exports sent to the US will be hit with a 12.5% tariff as the US president imposes levies on more than 80 nations.The Trump administration unveiled the latest tranche of tariffs for countries over their “failure to impose and effectively enforce a prohibition on the importation of goods produced with forced labour”.This, in our view, makes no sense.Forced labour is definitely something which needs to be combated around the world, so we support that objective.We absolutely need to see the strait of Hormuz be open and for freedom of navigation to happen there, for commercial shipping to be allowed through, and we very much call on Iran to allow that to be the case.We need to see a restoration to the normal global fuel supply chain because it’s having a significant impact around the world.Continue reading...

item.title: ‘Forced labour’ rationale for Trump tariffs met with bewilderment
item.author: Mark Sweney and agencies
item.content:encoded: Measures effectively replace blanket 10% tariffs Trump imposed in February that were struck down by US supreme courtDonald Trump has once again inspired anger and confusion in US allies and trading partners as he imposed a wave of tariffs on more than 80 countries, replacing an expiring global duty introduced earlier this year.The new measures effectively replace blanket 10% tariffs that Trump imposed in February. The US supreme court ruled many of those were illegal. The new levies come in at between 10% and 12.5% on countries including the UK, Mexico, Canada, Australia, India, China and the 27 countries that make up the EU.Continue reading...

item.title: Weather tracker: More than 900 wildfires rage across Canada
item.author: James Michelin for MetDesk
item.content:encoded: Fires remain out of control in Northwest Territories and Ontario, while Argentina endures record night-time heatRecent hot, dry weather in Canada has triggered hundreds of wildfires across the country. As of Wednesday, more than 900 were burning, scorching about 3m hectares of land. While fires have broken out in several provinces, the greatest concern is in the Northwest Territories and Ontario, where several remain out of control.Poor air quality has been reported across affected regions, with wildfire smoke carried by winds into the US and even across the Atlantic to northern Europe. The risk of further blazes remains high as North America enters the peak of fire season.Continue reading...

item.title: Heatwave and online shopping drive up retail sales in Great Britain
item.author: Mark Sweney
item.content:encoded: Hot weather and World Cup fever help defy analyst expectations of decline, with sales volumes up 1% in JuneOnline sales accounted for the highest level of retail sales in five years last month as record hot weather spurred a rush for air conditioning, outdoor products and clothing.The Office for National Statistics (ONS) said the volume of retail sales in Great Britain grew 1% month on month in June, as the hot weather and a World Cup retail rush defied analyst expectations of a 0.3% decline.Continue reading...

item.title: Regional mayors to take key roles in UK policy decisions as part of No 10 North plans
item.author: Pippa Crerar Political editor
item.content:encoded: Burnham says ‘no one in government loses when power is shared’ as he revives Brown-era national economic councilRegional mayors are to be given a leading role in the UK’s economic policy, Andy Burnham has said, as he called the opening of No 10 North “the biggest rebalancing of power our country has seen”.The new prime minister has revived the national economic council [NEC], first set up by Gordon Brown to put the government on a war footing during the 2008 financial crash, arguing that economic growth requires the same level of urgency, focus and collective action.Continue reading...

item.title: Donald Trump hits Australian exports to US with new higher trade tariff over claims of ‘forced labour’
item.author: Josh Butler and Sarah Basford Canales
item.content:encoded: Australia’s trade minister, Don Farrell, says tariff regime is unjustified and inconsistent with free trade agreement with USDonald Trump has hit Australian exports with new trade tariffs, claiming the country is not doing enough to combat forced labour, but the Albanese government has demanded he remove them immediately.The United States announced on Friday morning (AEST) that many Australian exports now attract a new 12.5% tariff as part of a broader regime affecting dozens of countries. However the Albanese government says Australia’s three largest export categories – including beef and gold – will avoid the tariff hit.Continue reading...

item.title: Australian regulator TGA warns of ‘rare but severe’ eye disorder linked to GLP-1 drugs like Ozempic
item.author: Achol Arok
item.content:encoded: TGA says product information for some diabetes and weight loss drugs updated with warning of NAION disorderAustralia’s medicines regulator has issued warnings for several type 2 diabetes and obesity-related treatments – including Ozempic and Mounjaro – over the potential to cause a “rare but severe” eye disorder that could lead to blindness.Continue reading...

item.title: ‘A small snippet’: what pubs make of Burnham’s £100m rates giveaway
item.author: Rob Davies
item.content:encoded: Landlords say high costs of running hospitality business outweigh potential savings of business rates discountThe policy, announced on Thursday, is part of a broader agenda to support the high street and will cost £100m.Continue reading...

item.title: Urgent need for more security after physical attacks and abuse, say MPs’ staff
item.author: Josh Halliday North of England editor
item.content:encoded: Exclusive: Westminster survey finds one in two staffers experiencing clinical levels of psychological distressStaff employed by MPs are calling for urgently tightened security after reports of some being physically attacked and hundreds receiving threatening calls and messages.A survey of more than 500 MPs’ staff members found that 78% had received threatening communication and nearly one in three fear for their own safety or that of colleagues.Continue reading...

item.title: Adelaide airport pulls ads from conservation groups but keeps gas and mining ads during ALP conference
item.author: Graham Readfearn Environment and climate correspondent
item.content:encoded: Digital billboards from environmental groups, targeting Labor MPs attending national conference, cancelled with airport stating they are ‘too political’Conservation groups have questioned why their advertisements targeting Labor politicians were pulled by Adelaide airport for breaking rules on political advertising while others promoting gas and mining kept running.Labor politicians including the prime minister, Anthony Albanese, have been arriving through the airport for this week’s ALP national conference from Thursday to Saturday.Continue reading...

item.title: India ‘cockroach’ activist Sonam Wangchuk ends hunger strike as protests spread
item.author: Hannah Ellis-Petersen in Delhi
item.content:encoded: Sonam Wangchuk halted strike amid concerns about ‘possible violence’ in India as protest movement urges demonstrations across country on Friday over police brutalityThe Indian “cockroach” activist Sonam Wangchuk has ended a 26-day hunger strike over an exam paper scandal that has spiralled into a wide ranging, youth-led wave of demonstrations against Narendra Modi’s government.Supporters meanwhile have called a nationwide day of protest on Friday.Continue reading...

item.title: Ireland urged to put pressure on Germany over pro-Palestinian activist’s trial
item.author: Deborah Cole Berlin correspondent
item.content:encoded: Irish politicians concerned about Daniel Tatlow-Devally’s treatment in jail want Micheál Martin to send observers to Ulm 5 trialA cross-party delegation of Irish politicians has urged Dublin to send observers to a high-security trial of pro-Palestinian activists in Germany and to raise concerns about the Irish defendant’s detention when the German chancellor visits next week.Mairéad Farrell of Sinn Féin, a member of the seven-member delegation, said she and her colleagues had attended the hearing in Stuttgart on Wednesday of the so-called Ulm 5 activists, who are facing charges over an attack on an Israeli arms company.Continue reading...

item.title: Middle East crisis is ‘getting out of control’, UN chief warns – as it happened
item.author: Jonathan Yerushalmy, Lucy Campbell, Maya Yang, Vivian Ho, Taz Ali, Siraj Datoo and Adam Fulton
item.content:encoded: This live blog has not closed, you can read our latest story from the region hereEarlier in the US day, before Iran’s Revolutionary Guard said it had stopped three oil tankers from passing through the strait of Hormuz, Donald Trump issued this threat on his Truth Social platform:From this point forward, any time the Islamic Republic of Iran shoots at a ship in the Strait of Hormuz, whether it be by Missile, Rocket, Drone, or any other device or weapon, the United States will bomb and destroy ONE BRIDGE OR POWER PLANT, including those located next to, or in, the Capital City of Tehran.Continue reading...

item.title: Democrats criticize Trump’s new tariffs as ‘end-run around the supreme court and Congress’ – as it happened
item.author: Robert Mackey, Shrai Popat, Lucy Campbell and Vivian Ho
item.content:encoded: This live blog is now closed.By a vote of 214-208 lawmakers in the House passed a war powers resolution to curb the Trump administration’s military action in Iran, ahead of the lower chamber’s August reccess.Four Republicans, Brian Fitzpatrick, Warren Davidson, Tom Barrett and , voted yes, alongside all Democrats.Continue reading...

item.title: The ‘cockroaches’ marching on India’s government – podcast
item.author: Presented by Helen Pidd with Hannah Ellis-Petersen; produced by Saskia Collette, Alex Atack and Brian McNamara; executive producer Eli Block
item.content:encoded: South Asia correspondent on the Cockroach movement – and the students driving India’s biggest protests in years‘I’ve never seen this many young people out on the streets,’ says .For days, tens of thousands of Indians have been marching under the banner of the Cockroach Janta party.Continue reading...

item.title: What to know about Trump’s new tariffs on more than 80 countries
item.author: Gaya Gupta
item.content:encoded: Tariffs ranging from 10%-12.5%, taking effect on Friday, are US president’s latest effort to circumvent CongressJust before a temporary levy on global imports was set to expire, the Trump administration issued a fresh wave of tariffs on more than 80 countries late on Thursday, ranging from 10% to 12.5%. The new tariffs take effect Friday morning.It’s Donald Trump’s latest effort to continue imposing global levies without congressional approval, despite the supreme court ruling in February that found the US president had illegally used his executive power to implement his global trade policies. Trump has long maintained that the US has been paying more than its fair share in tariffs, and that increased duties are necessary to boost American manufacturing and jobs.Continue reading...

item.title: Pentagon list of service members killed in Iran war removes most recent four
item.author: Robert Mackey
item.content:encoded: New York Times reports that list of those killed changed from 18 on Wednesday to 14 on ThursdayThe Pentagon’s official online list of service members killed in the ongoing Iran war does not include the four soldiers who died during renewed fighting over the weekend, despite the fact that their names were included in a Pentagon press release on the formal transfer of their remains back to the United States on Wednesday.A review of the current list on the Pentagon’s Defense Casualty Analysis System website shows that, despite having been last updated on Wednesday, the number of US military personnel killed during Operation Epic Fury, the Pentagon’s name for the US-Israeli war on Iran that Donald Trump launched on 28 February, remains at 14.Continue reading...

item.title: Prosecutors say blood stains on objects in D4vd’s home probably of victim
item.author: Uwa Ede-Osifo
item.content:encoded: Defense attorneys question LAPD criminalist on third day of hearing to decide whether singer will stand trialBlood stains on objects recovered from singer D4vd’s former Los Angeles residence probably came from Celeste Rivas Hernandez, the 14-year-old whose decomposed body was found in his Tesla last fall, according to testimony heard in court on Thursday.Prosecutors say D4vd, born David Anthony Burke, fatally stabbed 14-year-old Celeste Rivas Hernandez last year to prevent her from exposing the sexual nature of their relationship amid his rising profile in the music world.Continue reading...

item.title: Thousands flee wildfires in France and Spain as temperatures reach extreme levels in southern Europe
item.author: Jon Henley, Ajit Niranjan, and agencies
item.content:encoded: France asks EU for help while Spain declares national emergency around Madrid, as marine heatwave sees parts of Mediterranean Sea 5C hotter than normalBrutal heat torching southern Europe has forced about 30,000 people to flee wildfires in France and Spain as a marine heatwave scalds the Mediterranean and researchers warn that “Dantesque” scenarios cannot be ruled out as infernos worsen.France has requested assistance from ⁠the European Union to help it deal ⁠with a ⁠major ​wildfire near the country’s Atlantic coast, while Spain has declared a national emergency in the Madrid region and in the province of Avila, because of several wildfires burning out of control.Continue reading...

item.title: UK ‘ready to defend itself’ after Iranian threat over US bases
item.author: Hayden Vernon
item.content:encoded: IRGC claims base in south-west England was used by US to launch bombing missions, making it ‘legitimate target’The government has said the UK is “ready to defend itself” after Iran’s military warned that any bases being used by the US were “legitimate targets”.The UK has allowed the US to launch “defensive” operations from British bases hosting American planes since the start of its war on Iran but has refused to help in offensive operations.Continue reading...

item.title: Donald Trump says decision on ‘massive attack’ against Iran is close
item.author: Peter Beaumont Senior international correspondent
item.content:encoded: President’s remarks follow Houthi strikes on Saudi tankers in the Red Sea and oil prices rising past $100 a barrel againDonald Trump has said he is close to deciding whether to launch “a massive attack” against Iran, on a scale larger than strikes already witnessed during the months-long conflict with Tehran.Speaking to the Axios news site, the US president said: “I am considering a massive attack. Bigger than ever before. I am close to making a decision. We are all set for it.”Continue reading...

item.title: Mayor shot dead in Mexico months after surviving assassination attempt
item.author: Thomas Graham
item.content:encoded: Valentín Lavín Romero is the 14th mayor to be murdered since Claudia Sheinbaum became president in 2024A mayor was shot dead on Wednesday in the Mexican state of Morelos after having survived a previous assassination attempt earlier this year. He is the 14th mayor to be murdered since Claudia Sheinbaum became president in October 2024.Valentín Lavín Romero, who was from the Green party, an ally of the ruling leftwing Morena party, was wounded at the end of January when hitmen shot him at a petrol station on a motorway.Continue reading...

item.title: Ousted Ukrainian defence minister refuses any other role in Zelenskyy’s government – as it happened
item.author: Jakub Krupa
item.content:encoded: Mykhailo Fedorov says only in his former post could he ‘combat procurement corruption and complete the army’s transformation’EU foreign policy chief Kaja Kallas has offered a bit more detail on the new package of sanctions, saying they are “the largest round in four years,” and will “hit Putin where it hurts most.”“We have agreed on the 21st sanctions package against Russia.It delivers sweeping measures targeting Moscow’s financial system, its military-industrial complex and energy sector, that keep Russia’s war economy running. Continue reading...

item.title: Israeli security minister joins Jewish extremists as they storm al-Aqsa mosque compound
item.author: Emma Graham-Harrison
item.content:encoded: Far-right minister Itamar Ben-Gvir was among thousands who gathered at Jerusalem’s most sensitive holy siteIsrael’s far-right minister of national security, Itamar Ben-Gvir, joined thousands of Jewish extremists who stormed Jerusalem’s most sensitive holy site on Thursday.Escorted by armed police, they prayed, waved the Israeli flag and sang the national anthem in a provocative display of political power.Continue reading...

item.title: Two Russian men jailed in Angola for terrorism and spying
item.author: Rachel Savage in Johannesburg and agencies
item.content:encoded: The men were convicted over alleged plan to stage coup and organise deadly demonstrations over fuel prices last yearTwo Russian men have been found guilty by an Angolan court of terrorism and spying after being accused of planning a coup and helping to organise deadly demonstrations in the southern African country last year.Lev Lakshtanov and Igor Ratchin were sentenced to eight and 11 years in jail respectively. They had denied the charges, claiming they were in Angola for cultural and business activities.Continue reading...

item.title: MoD cancels army training in Kenya over powers to prosecute British soldiers
item.author: Hannah Al-Othman North of England correspondent
item.content:encoded: September exercise called off after Kenya raises concerns over funding and jurisdiction for criminal offencesThe British army has been forced to cancel training for its troops in Kenya after the Kenyan government refused to ratify a bilateral defence agreement.While the UK government wanted to renew the existing agreement, Kenyan lawmakers had sought changes to give local authorities greater powers to hold British soldiers accountable for any offences committed in the country.Continue reading...

item.title: Oil passes $100 a barrel again and shares slide as Middle East conflict escalates
item.author: Jillian Ambrose, Joanna Partridge and Richard Partington
item.content:encoded: Sharp rise in oil price reflects fears Houthi militias could strangle Saudi exports as US-Iran fighting intensifiesThe oil price broke above $100 a barrel for the first time in two months as a fresh escalation of the Middle East conflict threatens to compound disruption to global oil supplies.The benchmark oil price rose sharply on Thursday, from $95 the day before, amid fears that Yemen’s Houthi militia could strangle Saudi oil exports through the Red Sea while US-Iran tensions over oil flows through the strait of Hormuz intensify.Continue reading...

item.title: ‘Cockroach’ protests: India’s Modi promises to punish officials over exam leaks
item.author: Amrit Dhillon in Delhi
item.content:encoded: PM says ‘those who try to harm the future of our youth will not be spared’ as scandal spirals into widespread angerNarendra Modi has vowed to set up “fast-track courts” to speed up the punishment of officials accused of leaking examination papers, as the Indian prime minister seeks to quell a youth-led “cockroach” protest movement that has rocked Delhi and posed one of the biggest challenges to his government.In his first public remarks on the movement, Modi posted on X: “Nothing is more important than the welfare and future of our youth … Those who try to harm the future of our youth will not be spared.”Continue reading...
